cycleContext.note: May 30 scan (24-hour window since May 29 cycle). Key updates: (1) Hungary: Sulyok refused to resign; Magyar confirmed constitutional amendment process June 1 - OUTCOME DETERMINATIVE; (2) Oracle: WARN Act window now open, TechTimes June 1 confirms sign-release-or-forfeit-severance phase; (3) DRC Ebola: 282 confirmed cases as of June 1, no vaccine, NPR May 31 aid cuts hampering response; (4) OpenAI-DoD agreement published with classified network deployment and stated civil liberties prohibitions, contested by Lawfare; (5) Venezuela: NPR May 25 prisoner releases fall short, 500+ remain, UN repressive state apparatus intact; (6) India: 1,500+ Bengali Muslims expelled to Bangladesh without due process May-June 2026; (7) Tech sector: 142,000 layoffs in 2026 confirmed, Fortune May 31 MIT professor on AI-washing of layoffs; (8) Microsoft: Unit 8200 surveillance access terminated - first US tech company to withdraw services from Israeli military.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: RESOLVED: Sulyok refused to resign. Magyar confirmed constitutional amendment process June 1 (Bloomberg/WashPost/AlJazeera). Amendment process ~1 month. Venice Commission consulted. ASSESSABLE NOW.

ACTIVE: TechTimes June 1 confirms sign-release-or-forfeit-severance phase. Termination dates May 30-June 15. Algorithm-based remote classification novel legal question. New Jersey state WARN filed. ASSESS this cycle.

ESCALATING: DoD agreement now public. 42+ days since April 18 baseline. Full package: DoD agreement, FGF May 28, PBC conversion, mission-safety-word deletion, Kalinowski resignation, protest letters. OVERDUE.
ONGOING: Fifth week. Military deployed. No resolution. CNN May 29: protesters clashing. Four deaths confirmed. Economic crisis structural cause.
READY: Internet restored May 25. Amnesty confirms 39 political executions + 6,000+ arrests. Scale-floor methodology ruling required on 88-day shutdown + 80% of global executions.
ONGOING: HRW May 19 confirmed. UN Security Council May 2026. Aid down 37% Q1 2026. Score floor at 0; novel conduct sub-category documentation warrants assessment cycle.

prioritizedEntities.name: Bolivia
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Fifth consecutive week of general strike. Military fully deployed under May 27 law. Four deaths confirmed. CNN May 29: protesters clash with police. No resolution. Economic crisis (energy production collapse, USD shortage) structural cause. IBTimes: darkest hour.

prioritizedEntities.news_summary: HRW May 19: Israel curbs aid and kills civilians during ceasefire. UN Security Council May 2026: consolidate ceasefire, halt escalating violence. Aid volumes down 37% Q1 2026. 25 NGOs denied permit renewal. Authorized-resumption-with-systematic-denial pattern documented. Score floor at 0.

prioritizedEntities.name: Hungary
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Constitutional confrontation: Sulyok refused to resign by May 31 deadline. Magyar confirmed constitutional amendment process June 1 (Bloomberg/WashPost/AlJazeera/Euronews). Amendment to remove Sulyok takes ~1 month. Venice Commission consulted by Sulyok. Most significant ACC governance event since Magyar became PM. ASSESSABLE NOW.

prioritizedEntities.name: Democratic Republic of C
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Ebola (Bundibugyo strain, no approved vaccine): 282 confirmed cases, 42 deaths, 220 suspected as of June 1. WHO: catastrophic collision of disease and conflict. NPR May 31: aid cuts and misinformation hampering frontline response. 26.5M facing acute hunger. Contact tracing nearly impossible.

prioritizedEntities.name: Iran
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Internet restored May 25 after 88-day shutdown. Amnesty: 39 political executions + 6,000+ arbitrary arrests since Feb 28 US-Israel attack. Death toll 6,488-36,500. 2025: 2,159 executions (80% of global total). OHCHR HRC resolution extended FFM mandates. Scale-floor methodology ruling pending at composite 2.5.

prioritizedEntities.name: Sudan
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Entering lean season June-September with famine confirmed in El Fasher and Kadugli. 33.7M require assistance (globally highest). 19.5M acutely food insecure. 825,000 children under 5 expected severe acute malnutrition. 2026 plan only 16.2% funded. Civil war fourth year.

prioritizedEntities.name: Afghanistan
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: OHCHR Feb 2026: Taliban gender oppression and aid cuts pushing health system toward catastrophe. Women civil servants terminated without pay Jan 2026. Amnesty March 2026: new criminal regulation targets women with harsher punishments. Decree removes equality before law and authorizes domestic violence. HRW April 2026: Taliban restrictions haunting Afghan women abroad.

prioritizedEntities.name: Myanmar
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: 3.6M displaced (record). HRW 2026: junta airstrikes kill civilians, destroy schools/churches/medical facilities. Amnesty Jan 2026: junta atrocities surge 5 years since coup. Jet fuel on ghost ships linked to Iran. 12M face acute hunger. 982 civilians killed by airstrikes in 2025 (53% YoY increase). 16M require lifesaving assistance in 2026.

prioritizedEntities.name: Haiti
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: UN Gang Suppression Force: 400 Chadian troops deployed April 1 (first of 1,500). 5.8M (52%) face crisis-level food insecurity. 1,243 killed in 141 drone operations March 2025-January 2026 including 17 children. Sexual violence escalating. Security Council considering conversion to full UN peacekeeping operation.

prioritizedEntities.name: DeepMind/Google
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: CEO Hassabis warned of species-level transition with little margin for error at Stanford May 29. FSF third iteration published April 17 with Tracked Capability Levels. DeepMind UK workers launched union campaign demanding end to military contracts May 5. Whistleblower: Google AI assisted Israeli military drone surveillance.

prioritizedEntities.name: Turkey
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Democratic crisis ongoing. ForeignPolicy May 28: Erdogan designing his own opposition. TurkishMinute May 27: opposition turmoil may push Erdogan toward snap election. HRW documented attempt to sideline main opposition. Imamoglu imprisoned since March 2025. Wikipedia 2025-2026 Turkish protests actively updating.

prioritizedEntities.name: Venezuela
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: NPR May 25: prisoner releases fall short, 500+ remain per Foro Penal. UN FFM: 87 new politically motivated detentions since Jan 3. Amnesty bill lacks accountability mechanisms. UN: repressive state apparatus intact. Maduro arraigned Manhattan federal court Jan 5. 621 released as of March 8.

prioritizedEntities.name: Amazon
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: NLRB April 2026 bargaining order: Amazon must negotiate with Teamsters at JFK8. NLRB cleared path for federal court challenge. Amazon loses court fight over NLRB DSP process. TechTimes May 29: 142,000 tech layoffs in 2026. DSP driver organizing ongoing in CA, GA, IL, NY.

prioritizedEntities.name: India
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: India expelled 1,500+ Bengali-speaking Muslims from BJP-run states to Bangladesh without due process May-June 2026. AlJazeera May 29: how Modi efforts to isolate Pakistan backfired. UNSW May 2026: one year after brief war, close to another conflict? 64+ hate speech incidents against Muslims in first 10 days post-Operation Sindoor.

sectorAlerts.description: WARN Act window open as of May 30. TechTimes June 1: sign-release-or-forfeit-severance phase. Termination dates May 30-June 15. Algorithm-based remote-worker classification novel legal question. 14 workers filed allegations. New Jersey state WARN separately implicated. Composite 20.6 is 0.6pt above Critical boundary.

Magyar confirmed June 1 that Tisza Party will pursue constitutional amendment to remove President Sulyok after Sulyok refused to resign by May 31 deadline. Amendment process ~1 month. Venice Commission consulted. Bloomberg/WashPost/AlJazeera confirmed. Most significant ACC governance event in Hungary since Magyar became PM.

Ebola Bundibugyo (no approved vaccine, 25-50% fatality rate): 282 confirmed cases, 42 deaths, 220 suspected as of June 1. WHO: catastrophic collision of disease, conflict, and displacement. NPR May 31: aid cuts and misinformation hampering response. Contact tracing nearly impossible. Distinct from Zaire strain - different management protocol required.

May 7, 2026 political agreement delays Annex III high-risk AI rules (biometrics, critical infrastructure, employment, migration) to December 2027. Transparency obligations (Article 50) and GPAI rules remain August 2, 2026 (63 days from scan date). Colorado Anti-Discrimination in AI Law effective June 2026. Affects compliance framing for all AI Labs entities.
sectorAlerts.entities_potentially_affected: All ai-labs entities with EU market presence

sectorAlerts.description: TechTimes May 29: 142,000 tech workers laid off in 2026 as profitable companies fund $700B AI infrastructure. Fortune May 31: MIT professor - AI-washing of layoffs. DEI disclosures fell 65% in 2026. Meta (8,000), Oracle (30,000), Amazon (~30,000), Block (50% workforce). Structural sector-wide shift.

OCHA April 29, 2026 overview: 2,640 deaths from conflict Jan-March 2026; 1,179 security incidents. 2.8M internally displaced. 11.4M need critical food assistance. 3,794 schools and 974 health facilities non-functional. JNIM February 2026 coordinated attacks across eastern/northern Burkina Faso.

methodologyNoveCandidates: CONSTITUTIONAL-AMENDMENT-AS-EXECUTIVE-REMOVAL: PM using supermajority to remove head of state installed by predecessor via constitutional amendment (Hungary, Magyar vs Sulyok)
WARN-ACT-ALGORITHM-CLASSIFICATION-AVOIDANCE: Designating workers remote by algorithm to reduce WARN Act obligations then paid admin leave for exactly 60 days (Oracle)
NO-VACCINE-OUTBREAK-UNDER-AID-CUTS: Ebola Bundibugyo in conflict zone with no approved vaccine and humanitarian funding at record lows (DRC)
MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE: Rules for military AI use derived from bilateral vendor contracts rather than statute or regulation (OpenAI-DoD agreement, Lawfare May 2026)
